meta_title: For Students: Git with AI to Solve can't finish projects (Intermediate Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Git with AI to Solve can't finish projects
content.seo_description: Learn how to use Git with AI to overcome coding paralysis. A student-focused guide leveraging Antigravity and Claude Code for your portfolio projects.
content.answer_block: Stop fighting the 'unfinished project' syndrome by integrating Git with AI agents. By automating commit hygiene and using Claude Code to handle refactoring, you maintain momentum. This workflow ensures that every session ends with a clean, deployable state, turning overwhelming tasks into manageable, high-velocity technical milestones.
content.intro:
You stare at the blinking cursor, the weight of a half-finished repository looming over your portfolio. You have the vision, but the technical friction of managing state and debugging complex architectures has stalled your progress. It is the classic student trap: the graveyard of abandoned GitHub repos.

The secret to shipping isn't working harder; it is mastering the flow between your intent and your infrastructure. By pairing Git version control with the cognitive power of AI agents like Claude Code and Antigravity, you transform your workflow from a chaotic slog into a surgical, iterative process that guarantees completion.

steps.title: Architecting the Roadmap
steps.description: Before writing code, define the core feature set. Use Antigravity to break your MVP into distinct, atomic Git branches.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this project idea and break it down into 5 atomic milestones. For each milestone, provide a feature branch name and a summary of the required technical logic.

steps.title: Automated Semantic Commits
steps.description: Stop guessing commit messages. Use AI to ensure every Git commit follows Conventional Commits standards, keeping your history clean.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the current staged changes and generate a concise, conventional commit message following the format: type(scope): description.
steps.claude_code_command: claude commit -m 'feat(auth): implement jwt token generation'

steps.title: AI-Driven Debugging Loops
steps.description: When you hit a wall, don't quit. Use Claude Code to analyze the diff and identify the exact line causing the regression.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the recent diff in this branch. Identify the logic error that causes the test suite to fail and propose a fix.

steps.title: Documentation as You Build
steps.description: Documentation is often skipped. Use AI to generate README updates based on your recent commits.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Based on the git log for the last three commits, update the project README.md to reflect the new API endpoints added.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit README.md --instructions 'add documentation for the new auth endpoints'

steps.title: Final Merge and Cleanup
steps.description: Prepare your feature branch for the main codebase by squashing commits into a clean, professional history.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the history of this branch and suggest how to squash these commits into a single, cohesive feature unit.

pro_tips: Always commit before running an AI agent to ensure you can revert to a 'known good' state if the AI hallucinates a solution.
Use a .gitignore file generated by AI to prevent environment variables and node_modules from polluting your repo history.
Treat your Git history as a story for future employers; use AI to verify that commit messages describe the 'why' rather than the 'what'.
Run 'claude diff' before every commit to catch accidental deletions that could break your build.
Use branch protection rules on GitHub even for solo projects to force yourself to use Pull Requests, which adds a layer of review.
resources: Git Best Practices for Students: https://git-scm.com/doc
content.next_steps: Deploy your completed repository to Vercel or Netlify, then write a short blog post about the technical hurdles you solved during the process.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to stop starting and start shipping. Every line of code brings you closer to a job-ready portfolio. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
